title: Selection Sort on Integer Array
prompt_md:
## Goal

Sort an array of integers into **ascending** order using selection sort, then print the result.

## Input

- Line 1: an integer `n` (the number of elements, 1 <= n <= 20)
- Line 2: `n` space-separated integers (may be negative)

## Output

The `n` integers in ascending order, each followed by a single space (so there is a trailing space after the last value).

## Constraint

Implement the sort yourself with your own loops. Do **not** call `std::sort` or any other library sorting function.

title: Selection Sort a Vector of Structs by Field
prompt_md:
## Goal

Read a list of students (each with a name and a score), then sort them by score in **descending** order (highest score first) using selection sort. Print the sorted list.

A `struct Student` and the input scaffolding are already provided.

## Input

- Line 1: an integer `n` (number of students, 1 <= n <= 15)
- Next `n` lines: a name (a single word, no spaces) followed by an integer score

## Output

`n` lines, each containing the name and score separated by a space, ordered from highest score to lowest. All scores in the test data are distinct.

## Constraint

Implement the sort yourself with your own loops. Do **not** call `std::sort` or any other library sorting function.

test_cases.label: Input in ascending order forces a real sort
